title: ABC American Book Center
challenge: Inside The American Book Center and/or Paagman, find a book that has been banned, challenged, restricted, or removed somewhere in the United States. You may use Google or other sources to verify the book while you search. Document the following: Title and author, Where or by whom was it banned, challenged, restricted, or removed? This may be a school district, state, library system, parent group, law, or official review process. What idea in this book was someone afraid of? Think beyond the official reason. Was it queer identity? racial memory? women’s autonomy? grief? migration? sexuality? criticism of state violence? young people thinking for themselves? Upload a photo of the book in the shop using the following QR code.
clue to next stop: You have found one idea someone tried to remove from public life. Now walk toward the Spui and the Nieuwe Kerk: the churchyard where The Hague keeps one of its arguments about truth, exile, and belonging.
